projectDescription: A runtime-agnostic TypeScript framework for building headless, programmable AI agents with built-in sandbox execution, session persistence, and multi-target deployment (Node.js, Cloudflare Workers, CI/CD).
keyFeatures: Agent harness framework — write agents like Claude Code, but 100% headless and programmable
Virtual sandbox (just-bash) for fast, cheap, stateless agents with no container overhead
Remote sandbox connectors for full Linux environments (Daytona, E2B, Cloudflare Containers)
Persistent sessions backed by Durable Objects on Cloudflare or in-memory on Node.js
Skills and Roles — reusable Markdown instruction sets that shape agent behavior
MCP (Model Context Protocol) tool adapter for connecting to remote MCP servers
Multi-target builds: Node.js server, Cloudflare Workers, GitHub Actions, GitLab CI
Structured typed results from prompt/skill/task calls via Valibot schemas
Automatic context compaction for long-running sessions approaching token limits
CLI with dev, run, build, init, add, and logs commands
publicApiSurface: FlueContext — agent handler context (init, payload, env, req, log, id, runId)
init(AgentInit) → Promise<FlueHarness> — initialize harness with model, sandbox, tools
FlueSession.fs — FlueFs (readFile, writeFile, stat, readdir, exists, mkdir, rm)
Type — JSON Schema builder (re-export from pi-ai)
SandboxFactory / SandboxApi — connector interface for custom sandbox providers
flue dev / flue run / flue build / flue init / flue add / flue logs — CLI commands
triggers = { webhook: true } — HTTP endpoint trigger declaration
defineConfig({ target, root, output }) — flue.config.ts helper
